workflowPlannerDialogHint: Watch-folder: in-app (while the app runs) or Windows Task Scheduler (background tick). See Help — button next to the title.
workflowPlannerFieldBackendHint: In-app polls inside FluxAlloy. Windows: schtasks; macOS: LaunchAgent; Linux: systemd user timer — all with --workflow-watch-folder-tick.
workflowPlannerTaskSavedSchedulerWarn: Task saved, but Windows Task Scheduler registration failed (see log).
workflowPlannerEmpty: No tasks yet. Create a scenario in the builder, then Add task.
workflowPlannerAddHint: Type: folder watch. In-app backend — no OS scheduler registration yet.
workflowPlannerTaskInvalid: Check task fields (id, folder, scenario, interval 15–86400 s).
workflowScenarioBuilderDialogHint: The block diagram below mirrors the JSON. Run from watch-folder (runner) or the Scenario section in the editor FFmpeg rail.
editorWorkflowSectionHint: One click: run the ffmpeg chain on the file open in the editor (export settings from the scenario process block).
editorWorkflowScenarioEmpty: No scenarios — create one in Scenario builder
editorWorkflowRunOpenFileHint: Open a video in the editor first.
editorWorkflowRunErrorNoFile: No open file for the scenario.
editorWorkflowRunErrorPathDenied: No access to the file path.
editorWorkflowRunUrlQueued: Download queued; ffmpeg runs after yt-dlp finishes.
editorWorkflowRunUrlQueuedBusy: Download queued (row {rowId}); starts when the queue is free.
editorWorkflowRunUrlHint: Uses sourceUrl on the Download block; no open file required.
editorWorkflowRunUrlErrorNoSource: Set a valid http(s) URL on the Download block.
workflowTemplateLocalHint: Watch-folder or editor file, then process and save.
workflowTemplateUrlHint: sourceUrl on Download; yt-dlp then the same ffmpeg chain.
workflowScenarioValidateFail: JSON or scenario schema parse error.
